title: Weekly Briefing — Issue #1
tldr: The week in one idea: the industry pivoted to “the agent.” At Google I/O and across the labs, the unit of work shifted from a chat turn to a supervised fleet of agents — model + runtime + a consumer agent, shipped together.

Google I/O 2026 (May 19) went all-in on agents (~100 announcements): Gemini 3.5 Flash (frontier-ish at Flash price/speed), Omni (any-input→any-output), Antigravity 2.0 (agent-first dev platform), Managed Agents in the API, and Spark — a 24/7 personal agent.

Anthropic answered with enterprise infrastructure: self-hosted sandboxes (run agents inside your own VPC) and MCP Tunnels for private tool access — the data-residency story enterprises were blocked on.
DeepSeek made its 75% V4-Pro price cut permanent (May 24) — frontier-class open-weight reasoning at ~¼ the cost of comparable US tokens; brutal downward pressure on inference pricing.
Learn this week: writing Skills (markdown runbooks for agents), context engineering over prompt engineering, and sub-agent + planning orchestration — the durable leverage as models get stronger.

blocks.sub: From “a better chatbot” to “a supervised fleet of agents”
blocks.p: The throughline of the week — anchored by Google I/O on May 19 — was that every major lab now ships an agent stack, not just a model: a frontier model, a runtime to run it autonomously (sandboxes, managed agents, tunnels), and increasingly a consumer agent (Spark). The job is moving from line-by-line prompting to orchestration + verification.

list: Consumer agents go mainstream: Gemini Spark is the most direct challenge to ChatGPT yet — expect a wider rollout within weeks.
Compute stays THE bottleneck: the Blackstone–Google ~$5B TPU JV (May 18) is one more sign capital + power gate the frontier.
Open-weight price pressure intensifies: DeepSeek's permanent cut resets build-vs-buy math for high-volume workloads.

sections.h: 2 · Model & Capability Landscape — what to use for what
sections.intro: I/O reframed the competition around price/speed + distribution, not just top-of-benchmark. The practical map this week:

head: When you need…
Reach for
Why
rows: Frontier-ish quality at Flash price/speed (~⅓ the cost of comparable frontier models); powers Spark
Ambient 24/7 personal agent that reasons across your connected apps
75% cut now permanent — ~¼ the cost of equivalent US tokens
Multi-agent orchestration + VPC-local execution for sensitive data

blocks.note: Leaderboard scores vary by source — treat as directional. The week's story isn't a new #1; it's that the runtime around the model is now the battleground.

sections.intro: Study this section — it's where the compounding edge is as models get stronger.

blocks.sub: “Skills” — the highest-leverage thing to learn now
blocks.p: A Skill is a markdown file an agent loads for a specific task — an onboarding doc / runbook for an AI. Encode the procedure once; the agent follows it instead of you re-prompting. (Google also shipped Science Skills at I/O — the pattern is going mainstream.)
list: Write a runbook, not a question. Anticipate failure modes, say which tool to use when, and specify when to stop.
Structure with sections + tags (## instructions, ## tool_guidance, ## output) — models follow delineated structure far more reliably.
Include 2–5 canonical few-shot examples of the exact behavior wanted.

blocks.p: Treat context as a finite, precious resource. Smarter models need less prescriptive prompting but better context curation — give the agent exactly what it needs, when it needs it, not everything.

Force a planning phase before generation (practitioners report complex-coding success jumping from ~⅓ to ~⅔ just by planning first), and use specialized sub-agents with single jobs — one session doing everything causes context pollution.

list: Google Antigravity 2.0 — agent-first dev platform (desktop app, CLI, SDK, Managed Agents) with multi-agent orchestration.
Anthropic self-hosted sandboxes (public beta) — run agents inside your own VPC (Cloudflare/Daytona/Modal/Vercel day one) + MCP Tunnels for private tool access.
MCP (Model Context Protocol) — the standard way to connect agents to GitHub, Slack, Drive, etc. Wire up 1–2 MCP servers to your coding agent this week.
Gemini creative integrations — Adobe (Firefly), Canva (Magic Layers), CapCut now inside Gemini.
blocks.doIt: Pick one repetitive weekly workflow and turn it into a Skill + sub-agent. Best ROI move right now.

head: Move
Detail
Signal
rows: Capital + power remain the gating moat
Downward pressure on inference cost is structural, not promotional
Google pushing agents to billions of devices via Gemini/Android
Reach, not just benchmarks, decides consumer-agent winners

blocks.note: Fast-moving figures are directional. The durable pattern: capability is becoming table stakes; distribution + runtime + price decide outcomes.

checklist: Convert one recurring workflow into a Skill (markdown runbook + few-shot examples).
Add a planning phase to your agent prompts; split big jobs into sub-agents.
Wire up 1–2 MCP servers to your coding agent.
Pilot Gemini Spark (or ChatGPT agent mode) on one real personal workflow.
Re-run your inference cost math against DeepSeek V4-Pro's now-permanent price.
For sensitive data, evaluate VPC-local agent execution (Anthropic sandboxes) over vendor-cloud.

sources: blog.google (I/O 2026: Gemini 3.5 Flash, Omni, Spark, Antigravity, Science Skills) · CNBC · Anthropic (self-hosted sandboxes; MCP Tunnels) · DeepSeek · digitalapplied tracker · buildfastwithai daily roundups (May 18–24) · llm-stats.com (figures directional where noted).
